title: Ukraine Lands Armed Robot Ashore In Russian-Held Territory Via Drone Boat
summary: Ukraine has reportedly conducted a first-of-its-kind mission by landing an armed robot ashore in Russian-held territory using a drone boat. This innovative amphibious operation could signal a new direction for future military landings and robotic warfare tactics.

title: CENTCOM Deploys Saronic Corsair USV Against Iranian Submarine At Bandar Abbas
summary: U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) deployed three Saronic Corsair unmanned surface vessels (USVs) in a first-of-its-kind operation, attacking a laid-up Iranian midget submarine at the Bandar Abbas Naval Base. This marks the first combat use of maritime one-way attack drones by American forces.

title: Reditus readies first launch of its re-entry vehicle/hypersonic target
summary: Reditus is preparing for the inaugural launch of its ENOS spacecraft, which is being evaluated by the Missile Defense Agency. This vehicle will serve as a hypersonic target and testbed under the SHIELD contract, aiding in the development and testing of hypersonic defense systems.

title: Space tag: Jackal, Puma spacecraft to chase each other Victus Haze demo
summary: The Space Force's TacRS program is planning a Victus Haze demonstration where Jackal and Puma spacecraft will actively chase each other. This exercise aims to test and develop capabilities for on-orbit rendezvous, proximity operations, and potentially space domain awareness or counter-space operations.

title: Rafale Fighter Adds Cheap Drone Killing Rocket To Its Armory
summary: France has integrated laser-guided 68mm rockets onto its Rafale fighter jets. This provides a significantly cheaper option for engaging and neutralizing threats like Shahed-type drones, enhancing the fighter's cost-effective air defense capabilities.

title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
summary: iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote. This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a Snow Leopard […]

title: Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
summary: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to Siri. It actually works - which is big praise! - though it keeps things brief. Other betas available now […]

title: Panasonic’s PV-460 Camcorder Stabilized Shaky Videos
summary: If you grew up in the 1980s or ’90s, you likely remember shaky home video footage, taken with a handheld camcorder , of family gatherings, vacations, and other events. Camcorders combined a camera with a video recorder . They included a rechargeable battery, a slot for a videotape, and a shoulder strap. Most were …

title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
summary: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

title: First bird-scale robot to swim, dive, and launch back into flight
summary: A robot that swims like a diving bird and then flies like, well, a bird, sounds like the setup for two different machines bolted together. But researchers at MIT and EPFL built one that does both with a single set of wings. No propellers, no legs, and no origami-style folding mechanism to switch modes. Continue …

title: Inside the Skill Market: From Software Engineering Activities to Reusable Agent Skills
summary: arXiv:2607.09065v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Software engineering (abbrev. SE) has continuously evolved through increasingly powerful forms of reuse, from source code and libraries to components and services. Recent advances in AI agents have introduced a potentially new reusable artifact: skills. Emerging agent …
detail: This research explores a new paradigm in software engineering (SE) where AI agents can encapsulate and reuse specialized SE expertise as 'skills.' Historically, SE has advanced through increasing forms of reuse, from code libraries to components and services. With the rise of AI agents, the concept of a reusable 'skill' – a packaged piece of SE activity – is emerging, facilitated by agent skill repositories and marketplaces. This study conducts the first large-scale empirical analysis of these SE skills, systematically examining what activities they cover across the software development lifecycle, how they evolve, and how they are evaluated. The findings indicate a growing trend of SE activities becoming reusable artifacts via these skills. This development matters because it could fundamentally change how software is developed, making complex SE tasks more accessible and automatable for AI agents. By abstracting and sharing expertise as skills, developers could accelerate development cycles, improve consistency, and leverage advanced AI capabilities more effectively, opening new avenues for research in agent-driven software development.

title: QAgent: An LLM-based Multi-Agent System for Autonomous OpenQASM programming
summary: arXiv:2508.20134v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Programming quantum circuits at the OpenQASM level is essential for achieving hardware-aware optimization and reliable execution on noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices, yet it remains challenging due to the need for domain-specific planning, iterative …
detail: QAgent is introduced as the first autonomous multi-agent system, powered by Large Language Models (LLMs), designed to program quantum circuits using OpenQASM. OpenQASM is a low-level language crucial for optimizing and reliably executing quantum algorithms on current 'noisy intermediate-scale quantum' (NISQ) devices, but it is challenging to program due to the need for domain-specific planning and hardware-aware calibration. QAgent integrates a unified workflow of schema-aware task planning, example- and tool-driven code synthesis, and hardware-aware calibration. It leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to access structured quantum knowledge and employs coordinated multi-agent reasoning with iterative feedback to ensure correctness. The system significantly improves programming accuracy for quantum kernels. This innovation matters because it addresses a critical bottleneck in quantum computing: the difficulty of low-level quantum programming. By automating this complex process, QAgent can make quantum hardware more accessible, reduce programming errors, and accelerate the development and optimization of quantum applications, thereby bridging the gap between theoretical quantum algorithms and practical hardware implementation.

title: CogniConsole: Externalizing Inference-Time Control as a Formal Abstraction for Reliable LLM Interactions
summary: arXiv:2607.08774v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliability in large language model (LLM) systems is typically framed as a function of model capability. We challenge this by demonstrating that reliability is significantly influenced by \emph{inference-time control} -- the computational layer governing task framing …
detail: This research introduces CogniConsole, an architectural framework that challenges the conventional view that Large Language Model (LLM) reliability is solely a function of the model's inherent capability. Instead, it posits that 'inference-time control' – the computational layer governing how tasks are framed and context is selected – significantly impacts reliability. CogniConsole externalizes this control into a structured interface, combining programmatic coordination with bounded prompt-based reasoning. Through extensive experiments, the study demonstrates that increasing structural scaffolding in this control layer systematically reduces output variance and failure rates, even with a fixed LLM architecture. This suggests that many common LLM failure modes, such as context drift or inconsistent constraint adherence, stem from under-specified control rather than insufficient model capability. This work is significant because it provides an empirical basis for treating inference-time control as a first-class abstraction. By focusing on designing robust control mechanisms around LLMs, developers can create more reliable, predictable, and trustworthy AI systems, moving beyond simply scaling model size to achieve better performance in critical applications.

title: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls
summary: arXiv:2607.09076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cyberattacks on operational technology are increasingly causing costly downtime and physical damage, exposing the limitations of traditional rule-based monitoring in industrial IoT environments. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have strong semantic reasoning abilities …
detail: This paper introduces a 'neuro-agentic control framework' for autonomous cybersecurity defense in industrial IoT (IIoT) environments, addressing the limitations of traditional rule-based monitoring against sophisticated cyberattacks. The framework couples a Large Language Model (LLM)-based planner, which provides strong semantic reasoning, with a pre-trained Time-Series Foundation Model (TimesFM) to ensure physics-grounded control. A key innovation is 'Counterfactual Physics Injection,' a mechanism that simulates the impact of LLM-proposed interventions within the foundation model's numerical latent space before any physical actuation. This allows the system to identify and reject hallucinatory or unsafe actions, ensuring safety and reliability. Evaluated on an industrial dataset, the framework demonstrated superior performance compared to traditional baselines. This work is crucial because it tackles the challenge of safely integrating powerful, yet potentially unreliable, LLMs into safety-critical operational technology (OT) systems. By combining LLM intelligence with a robust, physics-aware safety mechanism, it enables autonomous defense against cyber threats that could otherwise cause costly downtime and physical damage in industrial settings.

title: Communication-Efficient Digital-Twin Coordination for Heterogeneous LLM Embodied Agents over Computing Power Networks
summary: arXiv:2607.09330v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Embodied agent teams powered by heterogeneous large language models (LLMs) are being widely deployed in physical artificial intelligence such as smart factories, warehouses, and service robotics. To enable collaboration among such an agent team, efficient coordination …
detail: This research proposes LDT-Coord, a communication-efficient framework for coordinating teams of heterogeneous Large Language Model (LLM)-powered embodied agents, such as those found in smart factories or service robotics. Existing coordination methods, which rely on multi-round natural-language conversations between agents, suffer from high communication overhead, limitations due to varying LLM capabilities, and action delays. LDT-Coord addresses these challenges by employing a lightweight digital twin (DT) server. Instead of direct natural language negotiation, each agent independently selects its intended action and reports both the action decision and structured temporal constraints over shared resources to the DT server. This approach effectively decouples coordination performance from the complexities of natural language interaction. This framework is significant because it offers a more scalable and robust solution for multi-agent collaboration in physical AI systems. By centralizing coordination through a digital twin, LDT-Coord enables more efficient resource management and reduces communication bottlenecks, paving the way for larger, more complex, and more reliable embodied agent deployments in real-world, resource-constrained environments.

title: Stop Over-Prompting: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.6 Guidelines Change Everything
summary: Forget the XML blocks and persistence scripts. OpenAI’s new prompting guide says define the destination, set the stopping conditions, and get out of the way.
